note: FDM self-evaluation score is 0.99998, but unified evaluator penalizes it to 0.9186 because cubic spline interpolation introduces artifacts in 2nd-derivative computation at h=1e-6 scale. The FDM grid solution itself is accurate (O(h^2)), but the pointwise evaluation interface required by the evaluator degrades its score.

Same spline interpolation penalty as 2nd-order FDM. Despite 4th-order accuracy on the grid (self-eval 0.99999), the evaluator's pointwise h=1e-6 finite differences on the spline surface yield nearly identical score to 2nd-order FDM.
Truncation to 5 Fourier modes introduces BC residual errors at high spatial frequencies. Score limited by Gibbs-like truncation artifacts.
Pure numpy MLP without autograd. Limited by numerical gradient accuracy and small network capacity. PyTorch-based PINN with autograd would likely score significantly higher.

Our analytical solution with 30 Fourier modes. Highest score among all methods. Demonstrates that analytical approaches maintain precision under pointwise evaluation, unlike grid-based numerical methods.
